topic: TabBar tab grows with title instead of fixed max-width; long titles must truncate with ellipsis (nowrap+text-overflow), tab width stays stable, + button stays put — pure CSS/layout constraint, zero behaviour change, pixel-fidelity to design reference
summary: The tab strip already has truncation machinery, but the width cap is bound to the wrong element. TabBar.css:88 caps the LABEL (.tabbar .tabs__tab-label) at max-width:200px, while the design's .tab cell — our .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper (Tabs.css:421) — carries no max-width and the design-fidelity-contract section 5 requires .tab max-width:220px. So each cell sizes to its content (method-chip + gap + label up to 200px + padding), grows with the title, varies with chip width, and exceeds the 220px target, pushing the + actions. The fix is pure CSS and .tabbar-scoped: add max-width:220px to the cell wrapper and remove the divergent label cap, reusing the base label ellipsis rule (Tabs.css:196) that already matches contract B1. deriveLabel returns a plain string, so no JS/markup/behaviour change is needed and existing tests stay green (the CT fidelity assertion that pins the old 200px label cap will need re-pointing to the 220px cell cap).

findings.relevance: The padded cell that the design contract calls .tab; has gap:8px/padding:0 10px 0 12px/border-right but NO max-width. Per contract section 5 it should carry max-width:220px — this is the fix site for the cell-level cap.

Caps the LABEL at max-width:200px instead of the cell at 220px; contract B1 specifies NO label max-width (only nowrap/overflow/ellipsis/flex). This mis-placed cap leaves the cell uncapped (varies with chip) and diverges in value (200 vs 220). Remove to align.

findings.surface: Base label ellipsis rule (.tabs__tab-label) — canonical pattern
findings.relevance: canonical pattern — reusable: flex:1/overflow:hidden/text-overflow:ellipsis/white-space:nowrap matches contract B1 exactly. Already present; fires once the cell is capped. No new truncation code needed.
flex:1 1 auto + min-width:0 on the button lets the label ellipsis fire once the wrapper width is capped. Already correct; supports the cell-cap fix with no further change.

flex:0 0 auto makes the tablist content-width; combined with wrapper flex-shrink:0 this is the runner-up framing's basis (flex-layer fix). Evidence shows the flex layer is intentional design (Fix C) and not the defect — the missing cell cap is.
Returns a plain string (name | method+url | Untitled); CSS owns truncation, no JS length cap. Confirms the fix is pure CSS with zero behaviour/markup change.
canonical pattern — reusable: project's existing max-width (Dropdown.css:39, 280px) + text-overflow:ellipsis truncation idiom; same pattern the cell cap reuses.

hypotheses.label: A
hypotheses.cause: Width cap is on the wrong element: .tabbar .tabs__tab-label caps the LABEL at 200px (TabBar.css:88), but the design's .tab cell (.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper, Tabs.css:421) has no max-width. So the cell sizes to content (chip+gap+label<=200+padding), grows with the title, varies with chip width, and exceeds the 220px contract target.
hypotheses.falsifier: If halting cell growth required a JS or flex change rather than a cell-level max-width, hypothesis A is wrong.

hypotheses.label: B
hypotheses.cause: Growth is a flex-distribution artifact: the content-width tablist (.tabbar .tabs__list flex:0 0 auto, Tabs.css:504) plus flex-shrink:0 wrappers, fixable at the flex layer (flex-basis/allow-shrink) rather than a max-width cap.
hypotheses.falsifier: Adding max-width:220px to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper alone halts growth and fires the ellipsis, making a flex-layer change unnecessary and non-contract-faithful.

hypotheses.label: C
hypotheses.cause: Only the value is wrong: bump the existing label cap from 200px to 220px on .tabbar .tabs__tab-label, leaving it on the label.
hypotheses.falsifier: Contract section 5 places max-width on the .tab cell, not the label; capping the label still leaves cell width = chip+label+padding, which varies with chip and exceeds 220px, failing the cell-level computed-style fidelity AC.

root_cause_hypothesis: The tab width cap is bound to the wrong element. The label is capped at 200px (TabBar.css:88) while the design's .tab cell (.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper, Tabs.css:421) carries no max-width, so the cell grows with content and diverges from the contract's .tab max-width:220px. Relocating the cap to the cell (and removing the divergent label cap) fixes growth and aligns to fidelity; the base label ellipsis rule (Tabs.css:196) already truncates.

approaches.name: Relocate the cap to the cell (contract-faithful)
approaches.description: Add max-width:220px to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper (Tabs.css:421 — the design's .tab cell) and remove the divergent max-width:200px from .tabbar .tabs__tab-label (TabBar.css:88), keeping the base .tabs__tab-label ellipsis rule. Pure CSS, .tabbar-scoped.
pros: Contract-faithful: caps the .tab cell at exactly 220px (design section 5)
Reuses the existing base ellipsis rule (Tabs.css:196) — no new truncation code
Tab width is stable, independent of method-chip width
Scoped to .tabbar — bare .tabs consumers (section tabs, PrimitivesDemo) untouched
Pure CSS, zero behaviour/markup change; unit tests stay green
Resolves the growth under every framing — no flex-layer change needed
cons: Touches two files (Tabs.css add + TabBar.css removal)
An existing CT fidelity assertion pinning the 200px label cap must be re-pointed to the 220px wrapper cap

approaches.name: Bump the label cap value (200 to 220 on the label)
approaches.description: Change .tabbar .tabs__tab-label max-width from 200px to 220px in place (TabBar.css:88); leave the cap on the label.
pros: No new rule added to the wrapper
cons: Diverges from contract section 5 — max-width belongs on the .tab cell, not the label
Cell width stays = chip+label+padding, varies with chip and can still exceed 220px
A cell-level computed-style fidelity AC on the wrapper would still fail

recommended_approach.name: Relocate the cap to the cell (contract-faithful)
recommended_approach.rationale: Reuses the canonical truncation rule already present at src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Tabs.css:196 (it matches design-fidelity-contract B1) instead of writing any new styling, and derives its authority from design-fidelity-contract section 5. The single cell-level cap resolves the tab-growth symptom under every enumerated framing, so no flex-layer change is needed. It satisfies unchanged_behavior: the change is .tabbar-scoped and CSS-only, so select/close/reorder, the dirty dot, the method chip, active styling, and the rendered text stay byte-identical; the sole visible difference is how an overlong title is clipped. Bare .tabs consumers are untouched. Runtime behaviour is deferred to the computed-style probe captured under Open Uncertainties before finalizing.

constitution_constraints.impact: Reuse the canonical base ellipsis rule at src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Tabs.css:196 (contract B1) instead of writing new truncation CSS; the fix only adds the missing cell max-width. Reuse beats reinvention.

Constrains the target: .tab (= .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper) max-width:220px, label nowrap/overflow/ellipsis, no label max-width. Computed-style AC asserts var(--token) as resolves-to-token; the 220px is the contract's resolved literal.
Keep the change CSS-only and .tabbar-scoped so bare .tabs consumers are untouched and select/close/reorder/dirty/chip/active behaviour is byte-identical.

complexity.codebase_notes: ~2 CSS edits across 2 files: add max-width:220px to Tabs.css:421 wrapper rule; remove max-width:200px from TabBar.css:88 label rule (keep ellipsis).
complexity.risk_notes: Scoped to .tabbar, CSS-only, no JS/markup change. Only regression surface: a CT fidelity assertion pinning the old 200px label cap.
complexity.verify_notes: Computed-style read (CT / Chrome DevTools MCP): assert .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper max-width=220px, label ellipsis triple, no cell growth with a long title, + button anchored.

next_step_text:
## Next step

Copy the block below into a new `/specify` session manually. No automation — user controls when (or if) `/specify` runs.

~~~
/specify "TabBar tabs flex-grow with their title text: a long title stretches the tab across the strip and pushes the new-tab (+) button. Tabs should hold a fixed max-width and ellipsis-truncate; tab width must stay stable and the + button stay anchored. — Cap the tab cell so it never grows with title length and long titles truncate with an ellipsis. Concretely: add max-width:220px to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper (the design's .tab equivalent), matching design-fidelity-contract section 5. The .tabs__tab-label ellipsis machinery (white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + flex:1) is already present and fires once the cap exists; the button already has min-width:0 + flex:1 1 auto. Tab width stays stable; +/spacer/overflow-chevron actions stay anchored. Computed-style fidelity AC targets per contract: wrapper max-width resolves 220px, padding 0 10px 0 12px, gap 8px, tab bar height 36px; var(--token) asserted as resolves-to-token (not literal hex)."

Research reference: research/YYYY-MM-DD-tabbar-tab-grows-with.md
Key facts:
- Mode: Enhancement
- Symptom: TabBar tabs flex-grow with their title text: a long title stretches the tab across the strip and pushes the new-tab (+) button. Tabs should hold a fixed max-width and ellipsis-truncate; tab width must stay stable and the + button stay anchored.
- Desired: Cap the tab cell so it never grows with title length and long titles truncate with an ellipsis. Concretely: add max-width:220px to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper (the design's .tab equivalent), matching design-fidelity-contract section 5. The .tabs__tab-label ellipsis machinery (white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + flex:1) is already present and fires once the cap exists; the button already has min-width:0 + flex:1 1 auto. Tab width stays stable; +/spacer/overflow-chevron actions stay anchored. Computed-style fidelity AC targets per contract: wrapper max-width resolves 220px, padding 0 10px 0 12px, gap 8px, tab bar height 36px; var(--token) asserted as resolves-to-token (not literal hex).
- Recommended approach: Relocate the cap to the cell (contract-faithful)
- Hypothesis addressed: A, B, C
- Hypotheses NOT covered: (none)
- Open uncertainties: 2 (see research doc §Open Uncertainties)
~~~

runner_up_framing.frame: Growth is a flex-distribution artifact, not a missing cell cap: the tablist is content-width (.tabbar .tabs__list flex:0 0 auto) and wrappers inherit flex-shrink:0, so the correct fix is at the flex layer (give tabs a flex-basis / allow shrink) rather than adding a hard max-width:220px to the wrapper.
runner_up_framing.falsifier: Design-fidelity-contract section 5 puts max-width on .tab (the cell), and once max-width:220px is added to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper the tab stops growing and the label ellipsis fires — a flex-basis change would be neither necessary nor contract-faithful.
